html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Twenty — Part Two

The Twelve Minor Prophets

YOEL AND AMOS

4QXII — Dead Sea Scrolls Scroll of the Twelve Prophets

Yoel Chapters 1-4 · Amos Chapters 1-9

The Locust Plague — I Will Pour Out My Spirit — Let Justice Roll Down — A Famine of the Word — I Will Restore the Booth of Dawid

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QXII — Dead Sea Scrolls — Cave 4 Qumran — The Scroll of the Twelve

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

YOEL — CHAPTERS 1 THROUGH 4 — THE DAY OF YHWH AND THE OUTPOURING OF THE SPIRIT

YOEL — CHAPTER 1 The Locust Plague — What the Cutting Locust Left the Swarming Locust Has Eaten — Lament Like a Virgin — The Day of YHWH Is Near

1 The word of YHWH that came to Yoel, the son of Petu'el.

2 Hear this, you elders. Give ear, all inhabitants of the land. Has such a thing happened in your days, or in the days of your fathers?

3 Tell your children of it, and let your children tell their children, and their children to another generation.

4 What the cutting locust left, the swarming locust has eaten. What the swarming locust left, the hopping locust has eaten. And what the hopping locust left, the destroying locust has eaten.

5 Awake, you drunkards, and weep. And wail, all you drinkers of wine, because of the sweet wine, for it is cut off from your mouth.

6 For a nation has come up against my land, powerful and beyond number. Its teeth are lions' teeth, and it has the fangs of a lioness.

7 It has laid waste my vine and splintered my fig tree. It has stripped off their bark and thrown it down. Their branches are made white.

8 Lament like a virgin wearing sackcloth for the husband of her youth.

9 The grain offering and the drink offering are cut off from the house of YHWH. The priests mourn, the ministers of YHWH.

10 The fields are destroyed, the ground mourns, because the grain is destroyed, the wine dries up, the oil languishes.

11 Be ashamed, O farmers. Wail, O vinedressers, for the wheat and the barley, because the harvest of the field has perished.

12 The vine dries up. The fig tree languishes. Pomegranate, palm, and apple — all the trees of the field are dried up, and gladness dries up from the children of man.

13 Put on sackcloth and lament, O priests. Wail, O ministers of the altar. Go in, pass the night in sackcloth, O ministers of my Creator, for the grain offering and the drink offering are withheld from the house of your Creator.

14 Consecrate a fast. Call a solemn assembly. Gather the elders and all the inhabitants of the land to the house of YHWH your Creator, and cry out to YHWH.

15 Alas for the day. For the day of YHWH is near, and as destruction from the Almighty it comes.

16 Is not the food cut off before our eyes, joy and gladness from the house of our Creator?

17 The seed shrivels under the clods. The storehouses are desolate. The granaries are torn down because the grain has dried up.

18 How the beasts groan. The herds of cattle are perplexed because there is no pasture for them. Even the flocks of sheep suffer.

19 To you, O YHWH, I call. For fire has devoured the pastures of the wilderness, and flame has burned all the trees of the field.

20 Even the beasts of the field pant for you because the water brooks are dried up, and fire has devoured the pastures of the wilderness.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Yoel as part of the Twelve. The locust plague of Chapter 1 functions simultaneously as covenant agricultural catastrophe and covenant theological sign: the four stages of locust devastation — cutting, swarming, hopping, destroying — strip the covenant land of every harvest. The covenant community's response is commanded: lament, fast, solemn assembly, cry out to YHWH. The covenant natural disaster is the covenant call to covenant repentance.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Yoel Chapter 1 in full. Verse 8 — lament like a virgin wearing sackcloth for the husband of her youth. The covenant grief metaphor: the intensity of the required lament is that of a young widow — the most acute grief the covenant community knows. The covenant land's loss of its agricultural covenant gifts is the covenant equivalent of the most devastating personal covenant loss.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 1. Verse 4 — what the cutting locust left, the swarming locust has eaten. The four-stage covenant destruction: each successive wave of locusts consumes what the previous wave left. The covenant completeness of the devastation is the covenant point — nothing survives four consecutive waves of covenant judgment. The covenant community is left with nothing to offer at the covenant sanctuary.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 14 — consecrate a fast. Call a solemn assembly. Gather the elders and all the inhabitants of the land to the house of YHWH. The covenant pastoral response to covenant catastrophe: Yoel does not theologize abstractly but gives concrete covenant instructions. The covenant community is directed through the covenant response process step by step: fast, assembly, gather, cry out. Covenant catastrophe has a covenant liturgical response.

YOEL — CHAPTER 2 Blow the Trumpet in Tziyon — A Great and Powerful Army — Return to YHWH With All Your Heart — I Will Pour Out My Spirit on All Flesh

1 Blow a trumpet in Tziyon. Sound an alarm on my holy mountain. Let all the inhabitants of the land tremble, for the day of YHWH is coming. It is near,

2 A day of darkness and gloom, a day of clouds and thick darkness. Like blackness there is spread upon the mountains a great and powerful people. Their like has never been before, nor will be again after them through the years of all generations.

3 Fire devours before them, and behind them a flame burns. The land is like the garden of Eden before them, but behind them a desolate wilderness, and nothing escapes them.

4 Their appearance is like the appearance of horses, and like war horses they run.

5 As with the rumbling of chariots, they leap on the tops of the mountains, like the crackling of a flame of fire devouring the stubble, like a powerful army drawn up for battle.

6 Before them peoples are in anguish. All faces grow pale.

7 Like warriors they charge. Like soldiers they scale the wall. They march each on his way. They do not swerve from their paths.

8 They do not jostle one another. Each marches in his path. They burst through the weapons and are not halted.

9 They leap upon the city. They run upon the walls. They climb up into the houses. They enter through the windows like a thief.

10 The earth quakes before them. The heavens tremble. The sun and the moon are darkened, and the stars withdraw their shining.

11 YHWH utters his voice before his army, for his camp is exceedingly great. He who executes his word is powerful. For the day of YHWH is great and very awesome. Who can endure it?

12 Yet even now, declares YHWH, return to me with all your heart, with fasting, with weeping, and with mourning.

13 And rend your hearts and not your garments. Return to YHWH your Creator, for he is gracious and merciful, slow to anger, and abounding in steadfast love, and he relents over disaster.

14 Who knows whether he will not turn and relent, and leave a blessing behind him, a grain offering and a drink offering for YHWH your Creator?

15 Blow the trumpet in Tziyon. Consecrate a fast. Call a solemn assembly.

16 Gather the people. Consecrate the congregation. Assemble the elders. Gather the children, even nursing infants. Let the bridegroom leave his room, and the bride her chamber.

17 Between the vestibule and the altar let the priests, the ministers of YHWH, weep and say, Spare your people, O YHWH, and make not your heritage a reproach, a byword among the nations. Why should they say among the peoples, Where is their Creator?

18 Then YHWH became jealous for his land and had pity on his people.

19 YHWH answered and said to his people, Behold, I am sending to you grain, wine, and oil, and you will be satisfied. And I will no more make you a reproach among the nations.

20 I will remove the northerner far from you, and drive him into a parched and desolate land, his vanguard into the eastern sea, and his rear guard into the western sea. The stench and foul smell of him will rise, for he has done great things.

21 Fear not, O land. Be glad and rejoice, for YHWH has done great things.

22 Fear not, you beasts of the field, for the pastures of the wilderness are green. The tree bears its fruit. The fig tree and vine give their full yield.

23 Be glad, O children of Tziyon, and rejoice in YHWH your Creator, for he has given the early rain for your vindication. He has poured down for you abundant rain, the early and the latter rain, as before.

24 The threshing floors shall be full of grain. The vats shall overflow with wine and oil.

25 I will restore to you the years that the swarming locust has eaten, the hopper, the destroyer, and the cutter, my great army, which I sent among you.

26 You shall eat in plenty and be satisfied, and praise the name of YHWH your Creator, who has dealt wondrously with you. And my people shall never again be put to shame.

27 You shall know that I am in the midst of Yisra'EL, and that I am YHWH your Creator and there is none else. And my people shall never again be put to shame.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 2 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The covenant pivot of Chapter 2 occurs at verse 12 — yet even now, return to me with all your heart. The Day of YHWH army of verses 1-11 — overwhelming, unstoppable, cosmic in scope — is suddenly suspended by the covenant invitation: even at this late moment, covenant return is possible. The covenant invitation interrupts the covenant judgment at its moment of maximum intensity.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 2 in full. Verses 28-32 in the Masoretic tradition — the outpouring of the Spirit on all flesh — appear in the Hebrew canonical tradition as Chapter 3 of the four-chapter Hebrew Yoel. The Orit preserves the full text including the Spirit-outpouring promise as a distinct covenant unit.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 2. Verse 13 — rend your hearts and not your garments. Return to YHWH your Creator, for he is gracious and merciful, slow to anger, abounding in steadfast love, and he relents over disaster. The covenant character formula drawn from Shemot 34:6 — the covenant self-disclosure to Mosheh after the golden calf — is here invoked as the covenant basis for covenant confidence in covenant return.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 25 — I will restore to you the years that the swarming locust has eaten. The covenant promise of covenant temporal restoration: not merely agricultural recovery but the covenant redemption of lost covenant time. The covenant years consumed by covenant judgment can be covenantally restored. The covenant arithmetic of YHWH's covenant mercy: years subtracted by covenant judgment are years added back by covenant restoration.

YOEL — CHAPTER 3 I Will Pour Out My Spirit on All Flesh — Your Sons and Daughters Shall Prophesy — Your Old Men Shall Dream Dreams — Before the Day of YHWH — Everyone Who Calls on the Name of YHWH Shall Be Saved

1 And it shall come to pass afterward, that I will pour out my Spirit on all flesh. Your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, your old men shall dream dreams, and your young men shall see visions.

2 Even on the male and female servants in those days I will pour out my Spirit.

3 And I will show wonders in the heavens and on the earth, blood and fire and columns of smoke.

4 The sun shall be turned to darkness, and the moon to blood, before the great and awesome day of YHWH comes.

5 And it shall come to pass that everyone who calls on the name of YHWH shall be saved. For in Mount Tziyon and in Yerushalayim there shall be those who escape, as YHWH has said, and among the survivors shall be those whom YHWH calls.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves this chapter as the third chapter of the four-chapter Hebrew Yoel, in close agreement with the Masoretic. The Spirit-outpouring promise of verse 1 is the covenant universalism of the covenant gift of prophecy: the covenant Spirit that was given to specific covenant individuals — prophets, judges, kings — will be poured on all flesh. The covenant democratization of the prophetic covenant Spirit reaches male and female servants alike.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves this chapter in full. Verse 1 — I will pour out my Spirit on all flesh — is read in the Orit tradition as the covenant fulfillment of Mosheh's covenant wish of Bemidbar 11:29: would that all YHWH's people were prophets. The covenant promise answers the covenant desire of the covenant mediator: the covenant Spirit will be distributed to the entire covenant community without mediation.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement. Verse 5 — everyone who calls on the name of YHWH shall be saved. The covenant universalism of the covenant Day of YHWH salvation: the covenant call is available to all — not restricted to the covenant people by ethnic covenant identity but open to everyone who covenantally calls on the covenant name. The covenant rescue from the Day of YHWH is covenant-name-based, not covenant-birth-based.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1 — on all flesh — on sons and daughters — on old men and young men — on male and female servants. The covenant catalog of recipients dismantles every social hierarchy of covenant spiritual access: age, gender, and social status are all overridden by the covenant Spirit's distribution. The covenant Spirit is not filtered through the covenant social hierarchy but poured out directly on every category of covenant community member simultaneously.

YOEL — CHAPTER 4 I Will Gather All the Nations — The Valley of Yehoshafat — Beat Your Plowshares Into Swords — YHWH Roars From Tziyon — Yehudah Shall Be Inhabited Forever

1 For behold, in those days and at that time, when I restore the fortunes of Yehudah and Yerushalayim,

2 I will gather all the nations and bring them down to the Valley of Yehoshafat. And I will enter into judgment with them there, on behalf of my people and my heritage Yisra'EL, because they have scattered them among the nations and have divided up my land,

3 And have cast lots for my people, and have traded a boy for a prostitute, and have sold a girl for wine and have drunk it.

4 What are you to me, O Tzor and Tzidon, and all the regions of Peleshet? Are you paying me back for something? If you are paying me back, I will return your payment on your own head swiftly and speedily.

5 For you have taken my silver and my gold, and have carried my rich treasures into your temples.

6 You have sold the people of Yehudah and Yerushalayim to the Greeks, removing them far from their own border.

7 Behold, I will stir them up from the place to which you have sold them, and I will return your payment on your own head.

8 I will sell your sons and your daughters into the hand of the people of Yehudah, and they will sell them to the Sabeans, to a nation far away, for YHWH has spoken.

9 Proclaim this among the nations: Consecrate for war. Stir up the mighty men. Let all the men of war draw near. Let them come up.

10 Beat your plowshares into swords, and your pruning hooks into spears. Let the weak say, I am a warrior.

11 Hasten and come, all you surrounding nations, and gather yourselves there. Bring down your warriors, O YHWH.

12 Let the nations stir themselves up and come up to the Valley of Yehoshafat, for there I will sit to judge all the surrounding nations.

13 Put in the sickle, for the harvest is ripe. Go in, tread, for the wine press is full. The vats overflow, for their evil is great.

14 Multitudes, multitudes, in the valley of decision. For the day of YHWH is near in the valley of decision.

15 The sun and the moon are darkened, and the stars withdraw their shining.

16 YHWH roars from Tziyon, and utters his voice from Yerushalayim, and the heavens and the earth quake. But YHWH is a refuge to his people, a stronghold to the people of Yisra'EL.

17 So you shall know that I am YHWH your Creator, who dwells in Tziyon, my holy mountain. And Yerushalayim shall be holy, and strangers shall never again pass through it.

18 And in that day the mountains shall drip sweet wine, and the hills shall flow with milk, and all the streambeds of Yehudah shall flow with water. And a fountain shall come forth from the house of YHWH and water the Valley of Shittim.

19 Egypt shall become a desolation and Edom a desolate wilderness, for the violence done to the people of Yehudah, because they have shed innocent blood in their land.

20 But Yehudah shall be inhabited forever, and Yerushalayim to all generations.

21 I will avenge their blood, blood I have not avenged, for YHWH dwells in Tziyon.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 4 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The Valley of Yehoshafat — YHWH judges — is the covenant geographic center of the cosmic judgment: all nations gathered for covenant judicial assessment of how they treated YHWH's covenant people. The covenant nations' treatment of the covenant people is the covenant criterion of their own covenant judgment. The reversal of the covenant people's scattering is the covenant context of the nations' covenant accountability.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 4 in full. Verse 18 — in that day the mountains shall drip sweet wine, and the hills shall flow with milk, and all the streambeds of Yehudah shall flow with water, and a fountain shall come forth from the house of YHWH. The covenant agricultural restoration imagery of Chapter 2:24-25 reaches its covenant eschatological fullness: the covenant land restored to overflowing covenant abundance, with a covenant fountain from the covenant sanctuary itself — echoing Yehezkel 47.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 4. Verse 10 — beat your plowshares into swords, and your pruning hooks into spears. The covenant inversion of the Yesha'yahu 2:4 covenant peace vision: where Yesha'yahu sees swords beaten into plowshares, Yoel commands plowshares beaten into swords — for the cosmic covenant battle. The covenant eschatological trajectory requires the covenant battle before the covenant peace.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 14 — multitudes, multitudes, in the valley of decision. The covenant decisiveness of the Day of YHWH: the Hebrew word translated decision — khharuts — means incision, decisive cutting. The covenant Day is not a time of covenant deliberation but of covenant finality. The nations gathered in the valley of decision are not deliberating — YHWH is cutting the covenant verdict against them. The covenant community is the covenant beneficiary of the covenant verdict.

AMOS — CHAPTERS 1 THROUGH 9 — JUSTICE RIGHTEOUSNESS AND COVENANT ACCOUNTABILITY

AMOS — CHAPTER 1 The Words of Amos — YHWH Roars From Tziyon — For Three Transgressions of Damascus — Gaza — Tzor — Edom — Ammon

1 The words of Amos, who was among the shepherds of Tekoa, which he saw concerning Yisra'EL in the days of Uzziyahu king of Yehudah and in the days of Yarov'am the son of Yo'ash, king of Yisra'EL, two years before the earthquake.

2 And he said, YHWH roars from Tziyon and utters his voice from Yerushalayim. The pastures of the shepherds mourn, and the top of Karmel withers.

3 Thus says YHWH: For three transgressions of Damascus, and for four, I will not revoke the punishment, because they have threshed Gil'ad with threshing sledges of iron.

4 So I will send a fire upon the house of Haza'el, and it shall devour the strongholds of Ben-Hadad.

5 I will break the gate-bar of Damascus, and cut off the inhabitants from the Valley of Aven, and him who holds the scepter from Beit-Eden, and the people of Aram shall go into exile to Kir, says YHWH.

6 Thus says YHWH: For three transgressions of Aza, and for four, I will not revoke the punishment, because they carried into exile a whole people to deliver them up to Edom.

7 So I will send a fire upon the wall of Aza, and it shall devour her strongholds.

8 I will cut off the inhabitants from Ashdod, and him who holds the scepter from Ashkelon. I will turn my hand against Ekron, and the remnant of the Pelishtim shall perish, says the Creator YHWH.

9 Thus says YHWH: For three transgressions of Tzor, and for four, I will not revoke the punishment, because they delivered up a whole people to Edom, and did not remember the covenant of brotherhood.

10 So I will send a fire upon the wall of Tzor, and it shall devour her strongholds.

11 Thus says YHWH: For three transgressions of Edom, and for four, I will not revoke the punishment, because he pursued his brother with the sword and cast off all pity, and his anger tore perpetually, and he kept his wrath forever.

12 So I will send a fire upon Teman, and it shall devour the strongholds of Botzrah.

13 Thus says YHWH: For three transgressions of the Ammonites, and for four, I will not revoke the punishment, because they have ripped open pregnant women in Gil'ad, that they might enlarge their border.

14 So I will kindle a fire in the wall of Rabbah, and it shall devour her strongholds, with shouting on the day of battle, with a tempest in the day of the whirlwind.

15 And their king shall go into exile, he and his princes together, says YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Amos in close agreement with the Masoretic. The covenant oracles against the nations of Chapters 1-2 follow the pattern of the three-four covenant formula — for three transgressions and for four — a covenant rhetorical device indicating the full covenant measure of accumulated covenant violation. Each nation is indicted for specific acts of covenant brutality: war crimes, slave trading, covenant treaty violation, perpetual covenant enmity.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Amos Chapter 1 in full. Verse 2 — YHWH roars from Tziyon and utters his voice from Yerushalayim. The same opening that closes Yoel Chapter 4:16 — YHWH roars from Tziyon — connects the two books within the 4QXII covenant scroll. The covenant roar from the covenant mountain is the covenant sound that precedes the covenant oracles against all the nations.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 1. Verse 9 — they delivered up a whole people to Edom, and did not remember the covenant of brotherhood. Tzor's covenant offense includes the violation of a specific covenant — the covenant of brotherhood — suggesting a formal covenant treaty between Tzor and the covenant people that Tzor violated. Covenant treaty violation is a covenant crime YHWH judges even among covenant nations.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1 — Amos, who was among the shepherds of Tekoa. The covenant pastoral identity of the prophet is unusual: Amos is not from a prophetic family or covenant institution but from the shepherd community of Tekoa in the Yehudah wilderness. The covenant prophetic call crosses social and geographic covenant boundaries: the southerner Amos is sent to the northern kingdom. The covenant word is not bound by the covenant prophet's own regional covenant community.

AMOS — CHAPTER 2 For Three Transgressions of Moav — Of Yehudah — Of Yisra'EL — They Sell the Righteous for Silver — You Made the Nazirites Drink Wine — I Will Press You Down

1 Thus says YHWH: For three transgressions of Moav, and for four, I will not revoke the punishment, because he burned to lime the bones of the king of Edom.

2 So I will send a fire upon Moav, and it shall devour the strongholds of Keriyot, and Moav shall die amid uproar, amid shouting and the sound of the trumpet.

3 I will cut off the ruler from its midst, and will kill all its princes with him, says YHWH.

4 Thus says YHWH: For three transgressions of Yehudah, and for four, I will not revoke the punishment, because they have rejected the Torah of YHWH, and have not kept his statutes, but their lies have led them astray, those after which their fathers walked.

5 So I will send a fire upon Yehudah, and it shall devour the strongholds of Yerushalayim.

6 Thus says YHWH: For three transgressions of Yisra'EL, and for four, I will not revoke the punishment, because they sell the righteous for silver, and the needy for a pair of sandals —

7 Those who trample the head of the poor into the dust of the earth and turn aside the way of the afflicted. A man and his father go in to the same girl, so that my holy name is profaned.

8 They lay themselves down beside every altar on garments taken in pledge, and in the house of their Creator they drink the wine of those who have been fined.

9 Yet it was I who destroyed the Amorite before them, whose height was like the height of the cedars and who was as strong as the oaks. I destroyed his fruit above and his roots beneath.

10 Also it was I who brought you up out of the land of Egypt and led you forty years in the wilderness, to possess the land of the Amorite.

11 And I raised up some of your sons for prophets, and some of your young men for Nazirites. Is it not indeed so, O people of Yisra'EL? declares YHWH.

12 But you made the Nazirites drink wine, and commanded the prophets, saying, You shall not prophesy.

13 Behold, I will press you down in your place, as a cart full of sheaves presses down.

14 Flight shall perish from the swift, and the strong shall not retain his strength, nor shall the mighty save his life.

15 He who handles the bow shall not stand, and he who is swift of foot shall not save himself, nor shall he who rides the horse save his life.

16 And he who is stout of heart among the mighty shall flee away naked in that day, declares YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 2 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The covenant rhetorical trap of Chapters 1-2 is constructed with covenant precision: the audience hears the covenant oracles against the surrounding nations with covenant approval, and then — verse 6 — the oracle turns against Yisra'EL itself. The covenant community that condemned the covenant violations of the nations is caught in its own covenant condemnation.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 2 in full. Verse 6-7 — they sell the righteous for silver, and the needy for a pair of sandals. Those who trample the head of the poor into the dust of the earth. The covenant economic injustice indictment of Yisra'EL is more specific than any of the nations' indictments: the covenant community's violations are covenant internal — they involve the exploitation of the covenant poor by the covenant wealthy.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 2. Verse 11-12 — I raised up prophets and Nazirites among your sons. But you made the Nazirites drink wine, and commanded the prophets, You shall not prophesy. The covenant silencing of the covenant voice: the northern kingdom actively suppressed both covenant prophets and covenant Nazirites — the two covenant institutions designed to maintain covenant counter-cultural covenant witness within the covenant community.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 9-10 — yet it was I who destroyed the Amorite before them and brought you up out of Egypt and led you forty years in the wilderness. The covenant historical recitation in the midst of the covenant indictment: YHWH grounds the covenant accusation against Yisra'EL in the covenant history of covenant gift. The more extensive the covenant gift received, the more severe the covenant indictment of covenant ingratitude. Covenant privilege intensifies covenant accountability.

AMOS — CHAPTER 3 You Only Have I Known of All the Families of the Earth — Do Two Walk Together Unless They Have Agreed — The Lion Has Roared — YHWH Has Spoken — The Altars of Beit-El Shall Be Punished

1 Hear this word that YHWH has spoken against you, O people of Yisra'EL, against the whole family that I brought up out of the land of Egypt:

2 You only have I known of all the families of the earth. Therefore I will punish you for all your iniquities.

3 Do two walk together, unless they have agreed to meet?

4 Does a lion roar in the forest, when he has no prey? Does a young lion cry out from his den, if he has caught nothing?

5 Does a bird fall in a snare on the earth, when there is no trap for it? Does a snare spring up from the ground, when it has taken nothing?

6 Is a trumpet blown in a city, and the people are not afraid? Does disaster come to a city, unless YHWH has done it?

7 For the Creator YHWH does nothing without revealing his secret to his servants the prophets.

8 The lion has roared. Who will not fear? The Creator YHWH has spoken. Who can but prophesy?

9 Proclaim to the strongholds in Ashdod and to the strongholds in the land of Egypt, and say, Assemble yourselves on the mountains of Samaria, and see the great tumults within her, and the oppressed in her midst.

10 They do not know how to do right, declares YHWH, those who store up violence and robbery in their strongholds.

11 Therefore thus says the Creator YHWH: An adversary shall surround the land and bring down your defenses from you, and your strongholds shall be plundered.

12 Thus says YHWH: As the shepherd rescues from the mouth of the lion two legs, or a piece of an ear, so shall the people of Yisra'EL who dwell in Samaria be rescued, with the corner of a couch and part of a bed.

13 Hear, and testify against the house of Yaakov, declares the Creator YHWH, the Creator of hosts,

14 That on the day I punish Yisra'EL for his transgressions, I will punish the altars of Beit-El, and the horns of the altar shall be cut off and fall to the ground.

15 I will strike the winter house along with the summer house, and the houses of ivory shall perish, and the great houses shall come to an end, declares YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 3 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The covenant logic chain of verses 3-8 is one of the most tightly constructed covenant arguments in the prophetic corpus: each rhetorical covenant question establishes a covenant cause-effect relationship, culminating in verse 8 — the lion has roared, who will not fear? YHWH has spoken, who can but prophesy? The covenant prophet's compulsion to speak is as covenant-logically inevitable as the prey's fear of the lion's roar.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 3 in full. Verse 2 — you only have I known of all the families of the earth. Therefore I will punish you for all your iniquities. The covenant logic of covenant election and covenant accountability is the most compressed and counterintuitive in the prophetic corpus: covenant election — you only have I known — produces covenant elevated accountability — therefore I will punish you. Covenant privilege and covenant responsibility are inseparable.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 3. Verse 7 — the Creator YHWH does nothing without revealing his secret to his servants the prophets. The covenant transparency of YHWH's covenant purposes: the covenant God does not act in covenant history without covenant announcement through covenant prophets. The prophetic corpus itself is the covenant evidence that YHWH has honored this covenant principle throughout covenant history.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 12 — as the shepherd rescues from the mouth of the lion two legs, or a piece of an ear, so shall the people of Yisra'EL who dwell in Samaria be rescued. The covenant remnant theology stated at its most devastating: the rescue of the shepherd who finds only fragments is not a rescue — it is the forensic evidence of what the lion did to the sheep. The covenant remnant that escapes Samaria is no covenant victory but the covenant evidence of total covenant defeat.

AMOS — CHAPTER 4 Hear This Word, O Cows of Bashan — I Gave You Cleanness of Teeth — I Sent Rain on One City and Not on Another — Yet You Did Not Return to Me — Prepare to Meet Your Creator

1 Hear this word, you cows of Bashan, who are on the mountain of Samaria, who oppress the poor, who crush the needy, who say to your husbands, Bring, that we may drink.

2 The Creator YHWH has sworn by his holiness that, behold, the days are coming upon you, when they shall take you away with hooks, even the last of you with fishhooks.

3 And you shall go out through the breaches, each one straight ahead. And you shall be cast out toward Harmon, declares YHWH.

4 Come to Beit-El, and transgress. To Gilgal, and multiply transgression. Bring your sacrifices every morning, your tithes every three days.

5 Offer a sacrifice of thanksgiving of that which is leavened, and proclaim freewill offerings, publish them. For so you love to do, O people of Yisra'EL, declares the Creator YHWH.

6 I gave you cleanness of teeth in all your cities, and lack of bread in all your places. Yet you did not return to me, declares YHWH.

7 I also withheld the rain from you when there were yet three months to the harvest. I would send rain on one city, and send no rain on another city. One field would have rain, and the field on which it did not rain would wither.

8 So two or three cities would wander to another city to drink water, and would not be satisfied. Yet you did not return to me, declares YHWH.

9 I struck you with blight and mildew. Your many gardens and your vineyards, your fig trees and your olive trees the locust devoured. Yet you did not return to me, declares YHWH.

10 I sent among you a pestilence after the manner of Egypt. I killed your young men with the sword, and carried away your horses, and I made the stench of your camp go up into your nostrils. Yet you did not return to me, declares YHWH.

11 I overthrew some of you, as when Creator overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah, and you were as a brand plucked out of the burning. Yet you did not return to me, declares YHWH.

12 Therefore thus I will do to you, O Yisra'EL. Because I will do this to you, prepare to meet your Creator, O Yisra'EL.

13 For behold, he who forms the mountains and creates the wind, and declares to man what is his thought, who makes the morning darkness, and treads on the heights of the earth — YHWH, the Creator of hosts, is his name.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 4 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The five-fold covenant refrain — yet you did not return to me — verses 6, 8, 9, 10, 11 — is the covenant structural backbone of the chapter: YHWH enumerates five successive covenant disciplines — famine, drought, blight, pestilence, overthrow — each intensifying, each followed by the same covenant non-response. The covenant disciplines were covenant invitations that the covenant community refused.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 4 in full. Verse 12 — prepare to meet your Creator, O Yisra'EL. The covenant invitation that has exhausted every covenant gentle approach: after five failed covenant disciplines, YHWH issues the covenant ultimatum. The covenant community that refused to return under covenant discipline must now face the covenant direct encounter without covenant preparation.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 4. Verse 1 — cows of Bashan, who are on the mountain of Samaria, who oppress the poor, who crush the needy, who say to your husbands, Bring, that we may drink. The covenant address to the wealthy women of Samaria who drive the covenant economic exploitation by their demand for luxury: the covenant chain of exploitation runs from the women's demand through their husbands through the poor who are squeezed to satisfy it.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 4-5 — come to Beit-El, and transgress. To Gilgal, and multiply transgression. The covenant irony of the covenant invitation to transgress: Amos parodies the priestly covenant invitation to worship — come and offer — by revealing its true covenant character. The covenant worship at Beit-El and Gilgal is not covenant approach to YHWH but covenant transgression in religious form. The covenant religious calendar — every morning, every three days — is the covenant intensification of covenant sin, not its remedy.

AMOS — CHAPTER 5 Hear This Word — A Lamentation — Seek YHWH and Live — They Hate Him Who Reproves at the Gate — Let Justice Roll Down Like Waters — Did You Bring Me Sacrifices in the Wilderness — I Will Take You Into Exile

1 Hear this word that I take up over you in lamentation, O house of Yisra'EL:

2 Fallen, no more to rise, is the virgin Yisra'EL. Forsaken on her land, with none to raise her up.

3 For thus says the Creator YHWH: The city that went out a thousand shall have a hundred left, and that which went out a hundred shall have ten left to the house of Yisra'EL.

4 For thus says YHWH to the house of Yisra'EL: Seek me and live.

5 But do not seek Beit-El, and do not enter into Gilgal or cross over to Be'er-Sheva. For Gilgal shall surely go into exile, and Beit-El shall come to nothing.

6 Seek YHWH and live, lest he break out like fire in the house of Yosef, and it devour, with none to quench it for Beit-El.

7 O you who turn justice to wormwood and cast down righteousness to the earth.

8 He who made the Pleiades and Orion, and turns deep darkness into the morning and darkens the day into night, who calls for the waters of the sea and pours them out on the surface of the earth — YHWH is his name.

9 Who makes destruction flash forth against the strong, so that destruction comes upon the fortress.

10 They hate him who reproves in the gate, and they abhor him who speaks the truth.

11 Therefore because you trample on the poor and you exact taxes of grain from him, you have built houses of hewn stone, but you shall not dwell in them. You have planted pleasant vineyards, but you shall not drink their wine.

12 For I know how many are your transgressions and how great are your sins — you who afflict the righteous, who take a bribe, and turn aside the needy in the gate.

13 Therefore he who is prudent will keep silent in such a time, for it is an evil time.

14 Seek good, and not evil, that you may live. And so YHWH, the Creator of hosts, will be with you, as you have said.

15 Hate evil, and love good, and establish justice in the gate. It may be that YHWH, the Creator of hosts, will be gracious to the remnant of Yosef.

16 Therefore thus says YHWH, the Creator of hosts, the Sovereign: In all the squares there shall be wailing, and in all the streets they shall say, Alas, Alas. They shall call the farmers to mourning and to wailing those who are skilled in lamentation.

17 And in all vineyards there shall be wailing, for I will pass through your midst, says YHWH.

18 Woe to you who desire the day of YHWH. Why would you have the day of YHWH? It is darkness, and not light,

19 As if a man fled from a lion, and a bear met him, or went into the house and leaned his hand against the wall, and a serpent bit him.

20 Is not the day of YHWH darkness, and not light, and gloom with no brightness in it?

21 I hate, I despise your feasts, and I take no delight in your solemn assemblies.

22 Even though you offer me your burnt offerings and grain offerings, I will not accept them, and the peace offerings of your fattened animals, I will not look upon them.

23 Take away from me the noise of your songs. To the melody of your harps I will not listen.

24 But let justice roll down like waters, and righteousness like an ever-flowing stream.

25 Did you bring to me sacrifices and offerings during the forty years in the wilderness, O house of Yisra'EL?

26 You shall take up Sikkut your king, and Kiyun your star-god — your images that you made for yourselves,

27 And I will send you into exile beyond Damascus, says YHWH, whose name is the Creator of hosts.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 5 in close agreement with the Masoretic. Verse 24 — let justice roll down like waters, and righteousness like an ever-flowing stream — is the covenant declaration that has become the most recognized verse in Amos and one of the most cited in the entire prophetic corpus. The covenant demand is for covenant structural justice — a permanent flowing stream, not an occasional covenant religious gesture.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 5 in full. Verses 21-24 — YHWH's covenant rejection of the covenant worship system in favor of covenant justice — I hate your feasts — I will not accept your offerings — take away your songs — but let justice roll down like waters. The covenant hierarchy is definitive: covenant justice is not an alternative to covenant worship but its covenant precondition. Covenant worship without covenant justice is covenant offense to YHWH.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 5. Verse 18-20 — woe to you who desire the day of YHWH. It is darkness and not light. The covenant reversal of popular covenant eschatology: the covenant community anticipated the Day of YHWH as the day of covenant deliverance for covenant insiders. Amos declares it the day of covenant darkness — the covenant community's covenant violations have transferred them from the covenant beneficiary side to the covenant judgment side of the covenant day.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 25 — did you bring to me sacrifices and offerings during the forty years in the wilderness? The covenant historical interrogation: the covenant community's forty-year covenant formation period in the wilderness was not defined primarily by covenant sacrifice. The covenant relationship in the wilderness was covenant-word-based, covenant-provision-based, covenant-presence-based. The post-settlement covenant community has replaced the covenant substance with covenant ritual — precisely inverting the covenant formation precedent.

AMOS — CHAPTER 6 Woe to Those Who Are at Ease in Tziyon — You Put Far Away the Day of Disaster — Shall Horses Run on Rock — I Will Raise Up a Nation Against You — From Levo-Hamat to the Brook of the Aravah

1 Woe to those who are at ease in Tziyon, and to those who feel secure on the mountain of Samaria, the notable men of the first of the nations, to whom the house of Yisra'EL comes.

2 Pass over to Kalneh, and see, and from there go to Hamat the great, then go down to Gat of the Pelishtim. Are you better than these kingdoms? Or is their territory greater than your territory?

3 O you who put far away the day of disaster and bring near the seat of violence,

4 Who lie on beds of ivory and stretch themselves out on their couches, and eat lambs from the flock and calves from the midst of the stall,

5 Who sing idle songs to the sound of the harp and like Dawid invent for themselves instruments of music,

6 Who drink wine in bowls and anoint themselves with the finest oils, but are not grieved over the ruin of Yosef.

7 Therefore they shall now be the first of those who go into exile, and the revelry of those who stretch themselves out shall pass away.

8 The Creator YHWH has sworn by himself, declares YHWH, the Creator of hosts: I abhor the pride of Yaakov and hate his strongholds, and I will deliver up the city and all that is in it.

9 And if ten men remain in one house, they shall die.

10 And when a relative, one who anoints for burial, picks up a relative to bring the bones out of the house, and he says to him who is in the innermost parts of the house, Is there still anyone with you? — he shall say, No. And he shall say, Silence. For we must not mention the name of YHWH.

11 For behold, YHWH commands, and the great house shall be struck down into fragments, and the little house into bits.

12 Do horses run on rock? Does one plow there with oxen? But you have turned justice into poison and the fruit of righteousness into wormwood —

13 You who rejoice in Lo-Davar, who say, Have we not by our own strength captured Karnaim for ourselves?

14 For behold, I will raise up against you a nation, O house of Yisra'EL, declares YHWH, the Creator of hosts, and they shall oppress you from Levo-Hamat to the Brook of the Aravah.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 6 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The covenant woe against the covenant comfortable of Tziyon and Samaria — verse 1-6 — is the covenant indictment of covenant prosperity theology: the covenant elite who lie on covenant ivory beds, eating covenant lambs, singing covenant songs, drinking covenant wine, anointing with covenant oils — and are not grieved over the ruin of Yosef. Covenant luxury without covenant grief over covenant community collapse is covenant callousness.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 6 in full. Verse 6 — they drink wine in bowls and anoint themselves with the finest oils, but are not grieved over the ruin of Yosef. The covenant diagnostic criterion for the covenant elite: not their wealth but their covenant indifference. Covenant prosperity is not the covenant offense — covenant anesthesia to the covenant community's covenant suffering is the covenant indictment.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 6. Verse 12 — do horses run on rock? Does one plow there with oxen? But you have turned justice into poison and righteousness into wormwood. The covenant nature argument: just as horses and oxen cannot function on rock — they need appropriate ground — the covenant community cannot function without covenant justice. Converting covenant justice into poison is as covenant-logical as trying to farm on bare rock.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 5 — who sing idle songs to the sound of the harp and like Dawid invent for themselves instruments of music. The covenant critique of covenant cultural production divorced from covenant reality: the northern kingdom's covenant cultural life — its music, its arts — is sophisticated but trivial, invented for covenant entertainment rather than covenant truth-telling. The comparison to Dawid is not covenant compliment but covenant irony: Dawid's music was covenant lament and covenant prayer; theirs is covenant distraction.

AMOS — CHAPTER 7 The Vision of Locusts — The Vision of Fire — The Plumb Line — Amos Told to Go Back to Yehudah — I Was No Prophet — YHWH Took Me From Following the Flock

1 This is what the Creator YHWH showed me: behold, he was forming locusts when the latter growth was just beginning to sprout, and behold, it was the latter growth after the king's mowing.

2 When they had finished eating the grass of the land, I said, O Creator YHWH, please forgive. How can Yaakov stand? He is so small.

3 YHWH relented concerning this. It shall not be, said YHWH.

4 This is what the Creator YHWH showed me: behold, the Creator YHWH was calling for a judgment by fire, and it devoured the great deep and was eating up the land.

5 Then I said, O Creator YHWH, please cease. How can Yaakov stand? He is so small.

6 YHWH relented concerning this. This also shall not be, said the Creator YHWH.

7 This is what he showed me: behold, the Creator was standing beside a wall built with a plumb line, with a plumb line in his hand.

8 And YHWH said to me, Amos, what do you see? And I said, A plumb line. Then the Creator said, Behold, I am setting a plumb line in the midst of my people Yisra'EL. I will never again pass by them.

9 The high places of Yitzkhak shall be made desolate, and the sanctuaries of Yisra'EL shall be laid waste, and I will rise against the house of Yarov'am with the sword.

10 Then Amatziyah the priest of Beit-El sent to Yarov'am king of Yisra'EL, saying, Amos has conspired against you in the midst of the house of Yisra'EL. The land is not able to bear all his words.

11 For thus Amos has said, Yarov'am shall die by the sword, and Yisra'EL must go into exile away from his land.

12 And Amatziyah said to Amos, O seer, go, flee away to the land of Yehudah, and eat bread there, and prophesy there.

13 But never again prophesy at Beit-El, for it is the king's sanctuary, and it is a temple of the kingdom.

14 Then Amos answered and said to Amatziyah, I was no prophet, nor a prophet's son, but I was a herdsman and a dresser of sycamore figs.

15 But YHWH took me from following the flock, and YHWH said to me, Go, prophesy to my people Yisra'EL.

16 Now therefore hear the word of YHWH. You say, Do not prophesy against Yisra'EL, and do not preach against the house of Yitzkhak.

17 Therefore thus says YHWH: Your wife shall be a prostitute in the city, and your sons and daughters shall fall by the sword, and your land shall be divided up with a measuring line. You yourself shall die in an unclean land, and Yisra'EL shall surely go into exile away from its land.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 7 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The three vision cycle — locusts, fire, plumb line — shows the covenant escalation of covenant judgment alongside the covenant intercessory success: the first two covenant visions are averted through Amos's covenant intercession. The third — the plumb line — is not averted. The covenant measurement has been taken, and the covenant community fails the covenant standard.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 7 in full. Verse 14-15 — I was no prophet, nor a prophet's son, but I was a herdsman and a dresser of sycamore figs. But YHWH took me from following the flock. The covenant autobiography of Amos: no prophetic credential, no covenant institutional connection, no covenant prophetic lineage. Only YHWH's covenant call from the ordinary covenant life of covenant agricultural labor. The covenant call is the only covenant authority Amos claims.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 7. Verse 10-13 — the confrontation between Amos and Amatziyah the priest is the covenant institutional conflict of the book crystallized: the covenant institution defends itself against the covenant word that threatens it. The priest's argument — verse 12-13 — this is the king's sanctuary, a temple of the kingdom — is the covenant fusion of covenant religion and covenant political power that Amos's entire ministry challenges.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 2 and 5 — How can Yaakov stand? He is so small. Amos intercedes twice with the same covenant argument: Yaakov's smallness. The covenant intercessor does not argue Yaakov's covenant righteousness — there is none to argue. He argues Yaakov's covenant smallness, appealing to YHWH's covenant mercy toward the weak. Twice YHWH relents. The third time — the plumb line — there is no intercession recorded. The covenant measure has spoken for itself.

AMOS — CHAPTER 8 The Vision of Summer Fruit — The End Has Come — They Who Swallow Up the Needy — I Will Make the Sun Go Down at Noon — A Famine of Hearing the Words of YHWH

1 This is what the Creator YHWH showed me: behold, a basket of summer fruit.

2 And he said, Amos, what do you see? And I said, A basket of summer fruit. Then YHWH said to me, The end has come upon my people Yisra'EL. I will never again pass by them.

3 The songs of the temple shall become wailings in that day, declares the Creator YHWH. So many dead bodies. They are thrown everywhere. Silence.

4 Hear this, you who trample on the needy and bring the poor of the land to an end,

5 Saying, When will the new moon be over, that we may sell grain? And the Shabbat, that we may offer wheat for sale, that we may make the ephah small and the shekel great and deal deceitfully with false balances,

6 That we may buy the poor for silver and the needy for a pair of sandals and sell the chaff of the wheat?

7 YHWH has sworn by the pride of Yaakov: Surely I will never forget any of their deeds.

8 Shall not the land tremble on this account, and everyone mourn who dwells in it, and all of it rise like the Nile, and be tossed about and sink again, like the Nile of Egypt?

9 And on that day, declares the Creator YHWH, I will make the sun go down at noon and darken the earth in broad daylight.

10 I will turn your feasts into mourning and all your songs into lamentation. I will bring sackcloth on every waist and baldness on every head. I will make it like the mourning for an only son and the end of it like a bitter day.

11 Behold, the days are coming, declares the Creator YHWH, when I will send a famine on the land — not a famine of bread, nor a thirst for water, but of hearing the words of YHWH.

12 They shall wander from sea to sea, and from north to east. They shall run to and fro, to seek the word of YHWH, but they shall not find it.

13 In that day the lovely virgins and the young men shall faint for thirst.

14 Those who swear by the Shame of Samaria, and say, As your god lives, O Dan, and, As the Way of Be'er-Sheva lives, they shall fall and never rise again.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 8 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The fourth vision — the basket of summer fruit — delivers the covenant wordplay: the Hebrew for summer fruit — kayitz — sounds like the Hebrew for end — ketz. The covenant visual pun is the covenant pronunciation of the covenant end. The ripeness of the fruit is the ripeness of the covenant judgment.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 8 in full. Verse 11-12 — I will send a famine of hearing the words of YHWH. They shall wander from sea to sea, seeking the word of YHWH, but they shall not find it. The covenant worst judgment: not famine of bread but famine of the covenant word. The covenant community that silenced the covenant prophets — Chapter 7:12-13 — will find in the covenant judgment that the covenant word has been withdrawn from them entirely.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 8. Verse 5 — When will the Shabbat be over, that we may sell grain? The covenant commercial desecration of the covenant calendar: the covenant community observes the Shabbat outwardly — the markets are closed — while internally counting the minutes until the covenant day ends so covenant commercial exploitation can resume. The covenant form is maintained while the covenant substance is being planned around.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 6 — they buy the poor for silver and the needy for a pair of sandals and sell the chaff of the wheat. The covenant commercial violations are specific and calculated: debt slavery for trivial amounts, selling what was supposed to be discarded grain waste as food. The covenant economic exploitation is not occasional but systematic — a covenant commercial system built on covenant extraction from those who have no covenant protection.

AMOS — CHAPTER 9 I Saw YHWH Standing Beside the Altar — Though They Dig Into Sheol — Are You Not Like the Ethiopians to Me — The Eyes of the Creator YHWH Are on the Sinful Kingdom — I Will Restore the Fortunes of My People

1 I saw YHWH standing beside the altar, and he said, Strike the capitals until the thresholds shake, and shatter them on the heads of all the people. And those who are left of them I will kill with the sword. Not one of them shall flee away. Not one of them shall escape.

2 If they dig into Sheol, from there shall my hand take them. If they climb up to heaven, from there I will bring them down.

3 If they hide themselves on the top of Karmel, from there I will search them out and take them. And if they hide from my sight at the bottom of the sea, there I will command the serpent, and it shall bite them.

4 And if they go into captivity before their enemies, there I will command the sword, and it shall kill them. And I will fix my eyes upon them for evil and not for good.

5 The Creator YHWH of hosts, he who touches the earth and it melts, and all who dwell in it mourn, and all of it rises like the Nile, and sinks again, like the Nile of Egypt.

6 Who builds his upper chambers in the heavens and founds his vault upon the earth. Who calls for the waters of the sea and pours them out upon the surface of the earth — YHWH is his name.

7 Are you not like the Kushim to me, O people of Yisra'EL? declares YHWH. Did I not bring up Yisra'EL from the land of Egypt, and the Pelishtim from Kaftor and the Arameans from Kir?

8 Behold, the eyes of the Creator YHWH are upon the sinful kingdom, and I will destroy it from the surface of the ground, except that I will not utterly destroy the house of Yaakov, declares YHWH.

9 For behold, I will command, and shake the house of Yisra'EL among all the nations as one shakes with a sieve, but no pebble shall fall to the earth.

10 All the sinners of my people shall die by the sword, who say, Disaster shall not overtake or meet us.

11 In that day I will raise up the booth of Dawid that is fallen and repair its breaches, and raise up its ruins and rebuild it as in the days of old,

12 That they may possess the remnant of Edom and all the nations who are called by my name, declares YHWH who does this.

13 Behold, the days are coming, declares YHWH, when the plowman shall overtake the reaper and the treader of grapes him who sows the seed. The mountains shall drip sweet wine, and all the hills shall flow with it.

14 I will restore the fortunes of my people Yisra'EL, and they shall rebuild the ruined cities and inhabit them. They shall plant vineyards and drink their wine, and they shall make gardens and eat their fruit.

15 I will plant them on their land, and they shall never again be uprooted out of the land that I have given them, says YHWH your Creator.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 9 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The final chapter of Amos moves from the covenant vision of inescapable covenant judgment — verses 1-4 — to the covenant promise of covenant restoration — verses 11-15. The covenant structure mirrors the book's arc: the same YHWH who commands the covenant destruction of the covenant sanctuary commands the covenant raising of the fallen booth of Dawid.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 9 in full. Verse 7 — are you not like the Kushim to me, O people of Yisra'EL? The covenant universal sovereignty declaration: YHWH's covenant governance of nations is not limited to the covenant people. The Kushim — the northeast African peoples — the Pelishtim — the Arameans — all moved by YHWH's covenant direction. The covenant election of Yisra'EL does not make them uniquely governed; it makes them uniquely accountable.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 9. Verse 11 — I will raise up the booth of Dawid that is fallen. The covenant restoration promise uses the image of a fallen sukkah — temporary shelter — for the Davidic dynasty: the royal house that had towered as cedar is now a collapsed booth. YHWH's covenant promise is to rebuild it — not to a temporary covenant shelter but as in the days of old.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 15 — I will plant them on their land, and they shall never again be uprooted out of the land that I have given them. The covenant permanence that closes the book: the covenant community that has been repeatedly uprooted — Egypt, wilderness wandering, exile — receives the covenant promise of covenant permanent planting. The covenant garden metaphor of Chapter 5:11 — they planted but shall not drink — is covenantally reversed: they shall plant and eat and never be uprooted again.

END OF PART TWO — YOEL AND AMOS
